block_02: Every four years, American citizens elect the U.S. president, while investors all over the world try to figure out how the outcome will affect their portfolios.
block_03: With this newsletter, we start an October series of emails dedicated to the upcoming Presidential Elections in the United States. Each email of the series will highlight a particular topic having a major impact on the U.S. political landscape in the past and present. In the first letter, we’ll observe the 2016 US elections and markets' reactions based on its results.
block_05: The 2016 US elections resulted in the Republican candidate Donald J. Trump’s victory. Such an outcome was indeed unexpected for most observers. Trump’s political and economic agenda was very different from the agenda of the Democratic nominee — Hillary Clinton.
block_06: Political issues raised by Trump within the election debates affected the global markets a lot. Considerable price movements were detected across all asset classes, including Forex pairs, stocks, and commodities:
block_09: Trump announced his intention to end free trade deals with Mexico and tax money sent home by immigrants
block_10: The Mexican peso fell sharply losing 13% of its value against the USD
block_11: Trump mentioned about imposing tariffs on goods imported from China and sanctions against companies that send their work overseas
block_12: By the end of the election day, Asian stock indexes closed in the red. While the Hang Seng in Hong Kong plummeted 2.2%, Japan's Nikkei dropped sharply by 5.4%
block_13: Investors tried to stabilize their portfolios by turning to assets like gold & the Japanese Yen that are widely seen as “safe havens” in times of political uncertainty
block_14: Gold surged 2% in price while JPY surged more than 2% against major currencies including the USD
block_15: Improving ties with Russia was also announced as one of the top priorities on behalf of Trump’s agenda
block_16: The main ruble-denominated benchmark of the Russian stock market MOEX Russia Index raised about 1.5%
block_17: Up next: The 2020 US presidential elections analysis, based on professional opinions
